\boxed{B}

Antonia’s mistake reflects **generalization**, where she incorrectly applies the label "cat" to a new object (rabbit) based on superficial similarities (e.g., both are animals or have similar shapes). This is a classic example of overgeneralizing a concept from one instance to unrelated ones. The other options (e.g., discrimination, assimilation) do not fit as well in this context.
